items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: Rosh Chodesh:  Sun. is first day of including L’David Hashem ori in Shacharis and Minchah. We also practise blowing shofar. Mon. is first day of blowing the shofar and saying three extra Tehillim. (on Mon. say Tehillim 1–3; on Tues. say Tehillim 4–6 and so on.)

items.verbatim: Note: We do not bentch the incoming month of Tishrei. The Alter Rebbe said in the name of the Baal Shem Tov, “Tishrei is blessed by the Holy One, blessed be He, Himself. And with the power of that blessing, the Jews bless the other eleven months of the year.”

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Nine Days - reduced joy from Rosh Chodesh Av
items.label: NOTE #1
items.verbatim: When the month of Av enters, we reduce our joy and minimise any business involvement that leads to joy.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #2
items.verbatim: Except for the clothes of young children, we don’t do any laundry, even if we will not be wearing the clothes until after Tisha b’Av.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Nine Days - daily siyum custom (Rosh Chodesh through 15 Av)
items.label: NOTE #3
items.verbatim: From Rosh Chodesh, up to and including 15 Menachem Av, it is customary to make or hear a siyum each day.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Nine Days - no meat or wine
items.label: NOTE #4
items.verbatim: During the Nine Days, we don’t eat meat or drink wine, except on Shabbos or at a Seudas Mitzvah.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Erev Shabbos Chazon - hot water/mikveh allowance
items.label: NOTE #5
items.verbatim: On Erev Shabbos Chazon (Friday 17 July), a person who washes with hot water and/or toivels himself in the mikveh every Friday may wash with hot water without soap (others do permit soap) and toivel himself on this Friday as well.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Shabbos Chazon - Alter Rebbe custom, Pirkei Avos ch.2
items.label: NOTE #6
items.verbatim: On Shabbos Chazon, the Alter Rebbe would wear all his customary Shabbos  clothing, without any deviation from his usual pattern. After Mincha we say the second chapter of Pirkei Avos.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Erev Tisha b'Av - Torah study restrictions from Chatzos, no Tachanun at Mincha
items.label: NOTE #7
items.verbatim: On Erev Tisha b’Av (Wednesday 22 July), all the restrictions that apply to Torah study on Tisha b’Av apply from Chatzot Hayom (12:01pm) onwards. We may study only the books of Eichah and Iyov, the unfavourable prophecies in the book of Yirmeyahu, Midrash Eichah and the description of the churban as recorded in the Talmud  Maseches Gittin 55b. At Mincha we do not say Tachanun.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #8
items.verbatim: At the meal before the Fast, we do not eat two cooked dishes. The prevailing custom is to eat a hard-boiled egg dipped in ashes. We do not drink any alcoholic drinks. Eating and drinking must be stopped by the time of Shkia.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Erev Tisha b'Av - Shkia, change footwear, Megillas Eichah/Kinos
items.label: NOTE #9
items.verbatim: At Shkia on Wednesday (5:09pm) we remove our leather shoes and put on non-leather footwear. For Maariv we sit on low chairs or cushions. Bring your own to shul. After Maariv we read Megillas Eichah and selected Kinos, then say veAtah Kadosh, a full kaddish, Aleinu and Kiddush Yatom.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av - shiva mourner attendance allowance
items.label: NOTE #10
items.verbatim: A person who is sitting shiva (chas v’shalom) may be in shul in the evening and morning until the Kinos are finished, because we are all in mourning on this day.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av morning - washing restriction on rising
items.label: NOTE #11
items.verbatim: Tisha b’Av: daytime.  Upon rising from sleep, a person should wash only his fingers, not his entire hand. If he has sleep around his eyes, and he would normally wash it off, he may do so on this occasion as well. We do not say the brocha “she’asah li kal tzarki” until the next morning.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #12
items.verbatim: We do not greet one another on Tisha b’Av.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av Shacharis - low chair, no tallis/tefillin, Kinos, Eichah
items.label: NOTE #13
items.verbatim: Shacharis: sit on low chair or cushion – bring your own to shul. No tallis or tefillin are worn, and we do not kiss the tzizis of the tallis katan. We say Kinos after the Haftorah. It is customary for each person to say Megillas Eichah individually after Kinos.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av - no work before Chatzos, food prep restriction
items.label: NOTE #14
items.verbatim: Until Chatzot Hayom (12:01pm) we don’t do any work that involves some time. Similarly we do not sit on regular chairs or prepare food for the evening meal until after Chatzot Hayom.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av - tallis/tefillin before Mincha, davening order
items.label: NOTE #15
items.verbatim: Before Mincha: we put on tallis and tefillin and say the appropriate brochos. Then the Shema, shir haYom, Ein Kelokeinu, the portion of Tehillim for the day and any shiurim which normally follow Shacharis. That concludes the omissions made in Shacharis. We then say the korbonos for Mincha, then continue with krias haTorah and Haftorah, followed by Shmonei Esrei etc.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #16
items.verbatim: We put on Rabbeinu Tam tefillin.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #17
items.verbatim: Ending the fast: We daven Maariv, wash negel vasser, wash our faces and rinse our mouths (no brocha).

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #18

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #19
items.verbatim: We can wear leather shoes again.

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.label: NOTE #20
items.verbatim: Breaking the fast of Tisha b’Av:  Refreshments at Tzemach Tzedek after Maariv, with a contribution by Gershon & Yona Gunsberger in honour of those members of their families who died al kiddush Hashem during the Shoah, and also in honour of Gershon’s alte-zeida Reb Yisroel ben Avraham Kalman haLevi Z”L, whose yahrzeit is on Tisha b’Av .

items.week_context: Some of the special features of the Nine Days including Tisha b'Av for 5786
items.occasion: 10 Av - meat/wine restriction continues until Chatzos
items.label: NOTE #21
items.verbatim: We do not eat meat or drink wine until after Chatzot Hayom on 10 Av (this year at 12:01pm). Similarly the other restrictions of the Nine Days remain in place until after Chatzot Hayom.

The week of Parshas Nitzavim: 21–27 Elul 5785 (14–20 Sept. 2025)
